components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: The γ-PGA fermentation medium contains 20 g/L glucose, 5 g/L NH₄NO₃, 11.35 g/L Na₂HPO₄, 8.15 g/L KH₂PO₄, 0.20 g/L MgSO₄⋅7H₂O, 0.01 g/L MnSO₄⋅H₂O, 0.0008 g/L CaCl₂, and 0.0045 g/L Na₂-EDTA.

components.name: ammonium nitrate
components.evidence: The γ-PGA fermentation medium contains 20 g/L glucose, 5 g/L NH₄NO₃ ... (provides both NH4+ and NO3-, 62.5 mM each)

components.name: O2 (air, shake/well-plate)
components.evidence: B. licheniformis were grown in twenty-four well plates with 2 mL working volume, and cultivated at 220 rpm and 37°C for 14 h.

media.base_medium: chemically defined γ-PGA production medium (glucose / NH4NO3 / phosphate-buffered, no complex components)

sources_chased.what: paper full text (Methods, media & culture conditions)

sources_chased.how: verified none exists — the paper defines the full recipe inline with no external base-medium citation, no named trace/vitamin stock

media.notes: Fully defined medium stated verbatim in the main text; recipe is complete (carbon = glucose; N = NH4NO3; phosphate buffer = Na2HPO4/KH2PO4; salts/trace = Mg, Mn, Ca; chelator = Na2-EDTA). No complex ingredients, no vitamins, no reductant — conditions are aerobic (220 rpm, 24-well plate), so target 'facultative'/O2 lb -10 should read aerobic. LB was used only as the seed/antibiotic growth medium for cloning, not as the fermentation medium. The current_components ion set already covers all elements; Na2-EDTA (a chelator, not an ion-exchange) is the only compound not representable as an existing exchange reaction.
